title: festuca ovina subsp. elata
intro: Festuca ovina subsp. elata, also known as mountain fescue, is a subspecies of grass belonging to the genus Festuca and is commonly found in mountainous regions. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Festuca ovina subsp. elata include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing, as well as its potential to improve digestion and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to take advantage of its medicinal properties and health benefits.
